candidate.description: Circuit breakers protect a caller from a failing downstream by tracking error rate over a sliding window and short-circuiting requests once a threshold is exceeded. After a configurable cooldown period, the breaker enters a half-open state, admitting a small probe of requests to test downstream health; success closes the breaker fully, while failure re-opens it for another cooldown. Modern implementations distinguish slow calls (latency > p99 target) from explicit failures, pair the breaker with a bulkhead to bound resource use during outages, and emit metrics for breaker state transitions so operators can correlate them with downstream incidents. The closed/open/half-open state machine is foundational to resilience libraries such as Hystrix, resilience4j, and Polly, and is a standard pattern in service mesh sidecars.

candidates_full.full_description: Resilience pattern that monitors downstream failure rate and trips into an open state to fail fast, then probes recovery via a half-open state before closing. Standard component of every resilience library.
Capping requests per unit time per key; complementary but not the same as failure-driven short-circuiting.
